title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every forest $H$, and every $k$, every $H$-free graph that does not contain a clique on $k$ vertices has bounded chromatic number.
context_text: The Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture is the central motivation for this paper's line of research. It is known when $H$ is a path (Gyárfás [6], the original paper proposing the conjecture), but remains open for general forests. The present paper strengthens the path case by proving polynomial bounds under exclusion of complete $d$-partite graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.5 (paths are multibounding): for every path $H$ and all $d \geq 1$ there is a polynomial $f$ such that every $H$-free graph with no $K_d(t)$ subgraph satisfies $\chi(G) \leq f(t)$. Taking $t=1$ recovers Gyárfás' path case of the conjecture; the result is strictly stronger and extends to brooms and disjoint unions of multibounding forests.
notes: Attributed via citations [6] (Gyárfás) and [20] (Sumner) in the paper's introduction.
paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VIII. Excluding a path and a complete multipartite graph

title: Conjecture 1.6
context_text: A graph $H$ is multibounding if for every $d \geq 1$ there is a polynomial $f(t)$ such that, for all $t \geq 1$, every $H$-free graph with no $K_d(t)$ subgraph satisfies $\chi(G) \leq f(t)$. Every multibounding graph must be a forest satisfying the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture, and any forest satisfying the polynomial-bound Conjecture 1.2 is multibounding. The paper establishes that paths, brooms, and disjoint unions of multibounding forests are multibounding, motivating the general conjecture.
notes: Explicitly introduced with 'We propose: 1.6 Conjecture' in the paper.
paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VIII. Excluding a path and a complete multipartite graph
